burstgpt_ceiling_analysis.finding: BurstGPT gap is cost-efficiency driven (−6% GPU-hours), not n_sla_safe driven. Conformal arrival-rate would recover at most +0.26% on BurstGPT. Low priority.

papers_reviewed.title: Beyond Prediction: Tail-Aware Scheduling for LLM Inference
papers_reviewed.reason: Per-request intra-instance scheduler; different decision layer than Aurelius (fleet autoscaler)

papers_reviewed.title: Scheduling LLM Inference with Uncertainty-Aware Output Length Predictions (TIE)
papers_reviewed.reason: Per-request SJF scheduler; log-t distribution insight could improve Erlang-C service time but requires token distribution telemetry not in Azure trace

papers_reviewed.title: Hermes: Efficient Serving of LLM Applications with Probabilistic Demand Modeling
papers_reviewed.reason: Multi-step application pipeline serving (PDGraph + Gittins); Aurelius serves single-request workloads

papers_reviewed.title: PARD: Proactive Request Dropping for Inference Goodput
papers_reviewed.verdict: PRIORITY CANDIDATE — BLOCKED BY FIVE-FAILURE RULE
papers_reviewed.reason: Directly maps to Aurelius's AdmissionPolicy stub; proactive admission control using remaining latency budget; +16-176% goodput on 64-GPU cluster; uses only decision-time telemetry available in Aurelius; requires Five-Failure Rule lift

papers_reviewed.title: Large-Scale LLM Inference with Heterogeneous Workloads: Asymptotically Optimal Control
papers_reviewed.reason: Asymptotic optimality proof for gate-and-route policies validates Erlang-C structure; full LP implementation requires heterogeneous GPU cluster telemetry not in current benchmarks

priority_next_experiments.blocked_by: Five-Failure Rule + human review of PR #83
priority_next_experiments.telemetry_needed: per-request remaining latency budget (available from SLA deadline - elapsed_time)

priority_next_experiments.experiment: Phase 1b-C energy overlay on serving traces
priority_next_experiments.telemetry_needed: Azure LLM 2024 timestamps mapped to hourly grid electricity prices
